0.name: Benign Lesion
precautions: Continue routine skin self-examinations monthly.
Use broad-spectrum SPF 30+ sunscreen daily.
Wear protective clothing and avoid peak UV hours (10am–4pm).
Schedule an annual full-body skin check with a dermatologist.
Monitor the lesion for any changes in size, shape, or colour.

1.name: Melanoma
precautions: Seek urgent consultation with a dermatologist or oncologist.
Do NOT attempt self-treatment or removal.
Photograph the lesion and document any changes immediately.
Avoid all sun exposure and use SPF 50+ sunscreen.
Request a dermoscopy and possible biopsy for definitive diagnosis.
Inform close family members — melanoma has a hereditary component.

2.name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
precautions: Consult a dermatologist promptly for clinical evaluation.
Biopsy may be required to confirm the diagnosis.
Avoid further sun exposure to the affected area.
Apply daily sunscreen (SPF 50+) and wear protective clothing.
Discuss treatment options: excision, Mohs surgery, or topical therapy.
Schedule regular follow-ups as recurrence is possible.

3.name: Actinic Keratosis / Intraepithelial Carcinoma
precautions: Seek dermatological evaluation — this is a pre-cancerous lesion.
Treatment options include cryotherapy, topical creams, or photodynamic therapy.
Strictly avoid sun exposure and apply broad-spectrum SPF 50+ sunscreen.
Wear UV-protective clothing, hats, and sunglasses outdoors.
Attend regular skin surveillance appointments every 6 months.
Quit smoking — it is a known risk factor for actinic keratosis progression.
